result:
This confirms `bioPipeReadJobCompList` (registered via `aeCreateFileEvent(server.el, ...)` in `bioInit`, so it runs on the **main thread's** event loop) is the only reader/mutator of `bio_comp_list` outside the worker thread. Good â€” I have everything needed.

## Shared state touched by `bioProcessBackgroundJobs` (background bio worker) and the main thread

`bioInit` (main thread) spawns one `bioProcessBackgroundJobs` thread per worker (`src/bio.c:172`). Here is every piece of state that crosses the thread boundary:

| State | Written by | Read by | Synchronization |
|---|---|---|---|
| `bio_jobs[worker]` (per-worker job list) | main thread via `bioSubmitJob` (`bio.c:185`) | bg thread (`bio.c:285,290,365`); also main-thread `bioPendingJobsOfType`/`bioDrainWorker` don't touch the list itself but wait on it | `bio_mutex[worker]` (`pthread_mutex_t`), held by both sides around every access |
| `bio_jobs_counter[type]` | main thread (`bioSubmitJob`, `bio.c:186`) and bg thread (`bio.c:366`) | main thread (`bioPendingJobsOfType`, `bio.c:376`) | `bio_mutex[worker]` â€” same mutex as above |
| `bio_newjob_cond[worker]` (condvar) | signaled by main thread (`bioSubmitJob:187`) and by bg thread itself (`bio.c:367`) | waited on by bg thread (`bio.c:286`) and by main thread in `bioDrainWorker` (`bio.c:388`) | Paired with `bio_mutex[worker]` (standard cond-wait pattern) |
| `bio_comp_list` (completion-notification list) | bg thread appends (`bio.c:351`); main thread (via `bioPipeReadJobCompList`, run on the event loop, registered in `bioInit`) swaps/recreates it (`bio.c:430`) | main thread reads length/contents (`bio.c:428-429`) | `bio_mutex_comp` (`pthread_mutex_t`), held on both sides |
| `job_comp_pipe[1]`/`[0]` (wakeup pipe) | bg thread writes byte (`bio.c:354`); main thread reads (`bio.c:424`, via the event loop) | â€” | Not lock-protected â€” safe by construction: pipe read/write are the OS-level sync primitive (best-effort wakeup; non-blocking, self-pipe trick) |
| `bio_threads[worker]` | main thread writes (`bioInit:177`); main thread also reads/clears in `bioKillThreads` (`bio.c:402-404`) | not read by the bg thread itself | **None** â€” but the bg thread never touches this array, so there's no cross-thread race on it (only main-thread-internal use) |
| `server.bio_cpulist` | main thread, set once at config load (immutable config, `config.c:3230`) before `bioInit` runs | bg thread reads once at startup (`redisSetCpuAffinity(server.bio_cpulist)`, `bio.c:267`) | **None** â€” safe only because it's an `IMMUTABLE_CONFIG` value fixed before the thread is created; no protection if that invariant were ever violated |
| `server.aof_bio_fsync_status` | bg thread (`bio.c:321,328`) | main thread in `aof.c:1064-1068`, `server.c:2422,5218-5220,6557` | **Atomic only** â€” declared `redisAtomic int` (`server.h:2309`), accessed exclusively through `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` macros (C11 `_Atomic`/`__atomic`/`__sync` builtins, relaxed ordering). No mutex. |
| `server.aof_bio_fsync_errno` | bg thread (`bio.c:322`, via `job->fd_args...` path) | main thread (`server.c:5220`) | Same â€” `redisAtomic int` (`server.h:2310`), atomic macros only |
| `server.fsynced_reploff_pending` | bg thread (`bio.c:329`) | main thread (`aof.c:1028,1161,1346,2898,3147`, `server.c:2050,2480`) | Same â€” `redisAtomic long long` (`server.h:2376`), atomic macros only |
| `bio_worker_title[]` | initialized once as a static array before any thread starts | bg thread reads its own entry (`bio.c:265`) | **None needed** â€” read-only after init, never written post-startup |
| `bio_job_to_worker[]` | static array, initialized at compile time (designated initializers) | read by both main thread (`bioSubmitJob`, `bioPendingJobsOfType`, `bioDrainWorker`) and bg thread indirectly (worker index passed in as `arg`) | **None needed** â€” immutable after compilation, never written |

**Summary of synchronization mechanisms in play:**
- `pthread_mutex_t bio_mutex[BIO_WORKER_NUM]` + `pthread_cond_t bio_newjob_cond[BIO_WORKER_NUM]` â€” protects each worker's job list and its counters/condvar.
- `pthread_mutex_t bio_mutex_comp` â€” protects `bio_comp_list`.
- The self-pipe (`job_comp_pipe`) â€” OS pipe semantics substitute for locking on the wakeup byte.
- C11/GCC atomic builtins via the `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` macros (`atomicvar.h`) â€” protect the three `server.*` AOF-fsync-status fields, all with relaxed memory ordering (no mutex, no explicit fence beyond what "relaxed" atomics provide).
- `server.bio_cpulist` and the two static arrays (`bio_worker_title`, `bio_job_to_worker`) and `bio_threads[]` have **no synchronization**, but are safe by convention: they're either write-once-before-thread-creation or never written by the background thread at all.
